subject: Gaming
title: Rogue: The Game That Named a Genre
description: A micro-lesson that makes Rogue: The Game That Named a Genre usable.

hook.text: Rogue used ASCII characters as graphics. Every dungeon was randomly generated. Every death was permanent.

buildup.text: Created in 1980 by two UC Berkeley students, Rogue ran on university mainframe computers.

discovery.text: Random generation meant no two playthroughs were alike. Players couldn't memorize solutions.

twist.text: The game was so influential that an entire genre—roguelikes—is named after it, decades later.

climax.text: Hades, Dead Cells, and Spelunky all descend from a 1980 text-based dungeon crawler.

punchline.text: ASCII graphics and random dungeons. An entire genre carries its name.

quiz.question: What genre is named after the 1980 game Rogue?
